description: Letter from Mūsā b. Yiṣḥaq b. Ḥisda, in middle Egypt, to Yosef b. ʿAwkal. In Judaeo-Arabic. Dating: Early 11th century. One of two letters (see also PGPID 4248) in which he urgently requests cash due to the challenges of the flax harvest and processing operations. There is competition for labor, with workers demanding advance payment for scutching flax and pounding madder, and he claims (in a friendly but urgent way) that his cash-flow problems are threatening the entire operation. He claims the workers are demanding premium wages and advance payment. He is also having difficulty finding a boat to ship his flax and madder bales via Busir. Moreover, the covering that the addressee sent for Avraham (who is sick) is too light and cheap. He is asked to send another one, but heavy this time (v11–13). MR/ASE
tags: illness letter 969-1517, Illness, madder, flax, Yosef b. Awkal
description_he: מכתב ממוסא בן יצחק בן חסדא, מצרים התיכונה, ליוסף בן עוקאל. על קשיי קציר הפשתן: תחרות על פועלים, דרישת מקדמות ואי-מציאת ספינה. כן מתלונן שהכיסוי שנשלח לאברהם החולה דק מדי.
